Plural Nouns & Adjectives (Ο Πληθυντικός)

Welcome to the A2 Stage! You have mastered the singular forms of nouns, adjectives, and verbs. Now it is time to multiply EVERYTHING.

In Unit 2, we peeked at the plural articles: - οι (The - for Masc & Fem subjects) - τους (The - for Masc objects) - τις (The - for Fem objects) - τα (The - for Neuter everything)

Now let's see what happens to the actual nouns and adjectives.

1. Masculine Plurals (Οι, Τους)

Masculine words are the most dramatic. They change their ending completely.

Singular Root: -ος, -ας, -ης Plural Root: -οι, -ες

Dictionary Form (Singular) Plural Subject (Nom) Plural Object (Acc)
ο φίλος (the friend) οι φίλοι τους φίλους
ο μαύρος (the black...) οι μαύροι τους μαύρους
ο πατέρας (the father) οι πατέρες τους πατέρες
ο μαθητής (the student) οι μαθητές τους μαθητές

[!WARNING] The Tricky "-οι" Ending Look closely at the top row. Both the article (οι) and the noun (φίλοι) end in an "i" sound. But when they become the Object, the article changes to τους, and the noun ending changes to -ους! - Οι καλοί φίλοι είναι εδώ. (The good friends are here.) - Βλέπω τους καλούς φίλους. (I see the good friends.)

2. Feminine Plurals (Οι, Τις)

Feminine words are wonderful because their plural endings never change between Subject and Object. The noun stays exactly the same; only the article changes!

Singular Root: -η, -α Plural Root: -ες

Dictionary Form (Singular) Plural Subject (Nom) Plural Object (Acc)
η μητέρα (the mother) οι μητέρες τις μητέρες
η καλή (the good...) οι καλές τις καλές
η κόρη (the daughter) οι κόρες τις κόρες
η ώρα (the hour) οι ώρες τις ώρες

Οι όμορφες γυναίκες είναι εδώ. (The beautiful women are here.) Βλέπω τις όμορφες γυναίκες. (I see the beautiful women.) Notice how nothing changed except the article (Οι ➔ Τις)!

3. Neuter Plurals (Τα)

Neuter words are the ultimate lazy words. Not only do their plural endings never change between Subject and Object, but their plural article (τα) never changes either!

Singular Root: -ο, -ι, -μα Plural Root: -α, -ια, -ματα

Dictionary Form (Singular) Plural Subject (Nom) Plural Object (Acc)
το βιβλίο (the book) τα βιβλία τα βιβλία
το καλό (the good...) τα καλά τα καλά
το παιδί (the child) τα παιδιά τα παιδιά
το μάθημα (the lesson) τα μαθήματα τα μαθήματα

🎯 Wrap-Up Checklist

Look at the sentence below and see if you can identify why every single word is spelled the way it is:

Οι καλοί πατέρες βλέπουν τα μικρά παιδιά. (The good fathers see the small children.)

  • Οι καλοί: Masculine plural subjects (-οι pattern).
  • πατέρες: Masculine plural subject (-ας becomes -ες).
  • βλέπουν: 3rd person plural verb ("They" see).
  • τα μικρά: Neuter plural object.
  • παιδιά: Neuter plural object (-ι becomes -ια).
